version_note: v12 — signal-model reconfiguration. Replaces the v11 nine-signal set with a tighter two-must-have + three-reinforcer + two-negative model. PRIMARY must-haves define an 85-point fit base: cash_pay_signal (weight 50, required_for_bullseye) and fertility_services (weight 35, required_for_bullseye, floor_tier Contender — a confirmed fertility practice is always at least Contender even on a thin score). REINFORCERS add fit on top of that base (not members of the denominator; fit caps at 100): iui_listed (+15), cycle_monitoring_listed (+10), patient_financing_visible (+10). NEGATIVES apply a flat 20-point penalty (full weight, not confidence-credited, subtracted after the 0.6/0.4 blend) and cap the tier at Contender: ivf_listed and rei_on_staff. REI is no longer a hard exclusion gate — it moved out of run_config into a negative scoring signal, and the 207VE0102X taxonomy pre-crawl skip was removed (run_config) so every record is crawled and scored. All signals are three-state (yes/no/not_found) with word-for-word textual anchoring and no inference; a yes without evidence_text + source_url is downgraded by the existing sourcing gate. Presentation-only column_label retained on cash_pay_signal (Cash Pay) and fertility_services (Fertility). The primary/reinforcer fit model is activated by the reinforcer:true flags and is read by the engine's _calculate_scores.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show, word-for-word, that the practice offers cash-pay / self-pay / elective services or patient financing? Answer "yes" ONLY when one or more of these terms appears verbatim in a services, pricing, billing, or patient-information context: "cash pay", "cash-pay", "self pay", "self-pay", "out of pocket", "out-of-pocket", "elective", "cosmetic", "aesthetic", "med spa", "medspa", "membership", "concierge", "package pricing", "financing", "CareCredit", or "hormone optimization". Answer "no" ONLY when the page explicitly states cash-pay/self-pay is not available (e.g. "insurance required"). Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer from the mere presence of services; the term must appear word-for-word. Quote the exact matched phrase in evidence_text and put the page URL it came from in source_url.

Does the website show, word-for-word, that the practice offers fertility or infertility services? Answer "yes" ONLY when one or more of these terms appears verbatim as a service or clinical offering: "fertility", "infertility", "trying to conceive", "TTC", "preconception", "ovulation induction", "reproductive health", "fertility evaluation", or "fertility workup". DISAMBIGUATION: a bare "fertility specialist" with NO reproductive-endocrinology credential language counts toward THIS signal (yes), not toward rei_on_staff. Answer "no" ONLY when the page explicitly states the practice does not provide fertility care. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer. Quote the exact matched phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.

Does the website list IUI as an offered procedure? Answer "yes" ONLY when "IUI", "intrauterine insemination", or "artificial insemination" appears verbatim as a service or procedure. Answer "no" ONLY when the page states IUI is not offered. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer from generic fertility language. Quote the exact phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.

Does the website list fertility cycle monitoring as a service? Answer "yes" ONLY when one of these appears verbatim: "cycle monitoring", "follicle monitoring", "follicle tracking", "monitoring ultrasound", "serial ultrasound", or "ovulation tracking". Answer "no" ONLY when the page states it is not offered. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT match the bare word "ultrasound" or "monitoring" alone. Quote the exact phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.

Does the website show, word-for-word, a third-party or in-house patient financing option? Answer "yes" ONLY when one of these appears verbatim: "CareCredit", "Cherry", "Sunbit", "patient financing", "financing available", "apply for financing", "payment plan", "payment plans", or "monthly payments". Answer "no" ONLY when the page states financing is not available. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer. Quote the exact phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.

Does the practice offer IVF (in vitro fertilization) as an in-house service? Answer "yes" ONLY when one of these appears verbatim as a listed service or practice identity: "IVF", "in vitro fertilization", "embryo transfer", "egg retrieval", or "ICSI". REFER-VS-OFFER GUARD: "we refer for IVF" or "referral for IVF" is NOT a yes (answer not_found) — only an in-house IVF offering counts. Answer "no" ONLY when the page explicitly states IVF is not offered. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer. Quote the exact phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.

Does the practice have a reproductive endocrinologist (REI) on staff or identify as an REI practice? Answer "yes" ONLY when one of these appears verbatim: "reproductive endocrinologist", "REI", "board-certified in reproductive endocrinology", or "reproductive endocrinology and infertility". DISAMBIGUATION: a bare "fertility specialist" with NO REI credential language is NOT a yes here (it belongs to fertility_services) — this signal fires ONLY on the explicit REI terms above. Answer "no" ONLY when the page states there is no REI on staff. Otherwise answer "not_found". Do NOT infer. Quote the exact phrase in evidence_text and the page URL in source_url.
